title: Introducing Grok 4.5
excerpt: xAI launches Grok 4.5, described as an Opus-class model but faster and cheaper ($2/$6 per M tokens), trained alongside Cursor for coding and agentic tasks; not yet available in the EU.
filter_reason: Major model release from xAI with concrete capability and pricing claims - squarely in scope.
summary: xAI launched Grok 4.5, pitching it as an Opus-class model that is faster and cheaper at $2/$6 per million tokens. Notably, xAI says it was trained alongside Cursor with a focus on coding and agentic tasks, signaling a deliberate play for the coding-agent market rather than general chat. It is not yet available in the EU. If the Opus-class claim holds, the price point puts real pressure on frontier coding-model pricing.
